text: The fish called by the ancients callionymus, or uranoscopus, was supposed to be a specific in cases of leucoma; it was, however, a salt-water fish. In verses 1-9 the other versions vary hardly at all, but from this point onward they shew considerable difference, and have nothing corresponding to verses 16-22 of the Vulgate.
The meaning of this mystical allusion is unknown.
